name: people-intelligence
goal: Enrich people profiles with stances (LLM-extracted), bidirectional action items (regex-extracted with lifecycle), and relationship health metrics. Transform meeting prep from file matching to genuine people intelligence with source citations.

userStories.title: Extract person-memory module from entity.ts
userStories.description: Move signal collection, aggregation, rendering, and upsert functions into packages/core/src/services/person-memory.ts. Zero behavior change, pure refactor. Functions to extract: collectSignalsForPerson(), aggregateSignals(), renderPersonMemorySection(), upsertPersonMemorySection(), extractPersonMemorySection(), getPersonMemoryLastRefreshed(), isMemoryStale(), normalizeSignalTopic(), types PersonMemorySignal, AggregatedPersonSignal, RefreshPersonMemoryInternalOptions, constants AUTO_PERSON_MEMORY_START/END. Read packages/core/src/services/entity.ts, packages/core/src/services/LEARNINGS.md, and packages/core/test/services/person-memory.test.ts before starting.
acceptanceCriteria: entity.ts drops to approximately 1200 lines
person-memory.ts exists with all extracted functions exported
Direct unit tests for collectSignalsForPerson(), aggregateSignals(), upsertPersonMemorySection() added to packages/core/test/services/person-memory-unit.test.ts
All existing person-memory.test.ts integration tests pass unchanged
npm run typecheck && npm test pass

userStories.title: Build LLM stance extraction module
userStories.description: Create packages/core/src/services/person-signals.ts. Export buildStancePrompt(content, personName), parseStanceResponse(response), extractStancesForPerson(content, personName, callLLM) following extractInsights() pattern from packages/core/src/integrations/conversations/extract.ts. Define PersonStance type: { topic, direction (supports|opposes|concerned|neutral), summary, evidenceQuote, source, date }. LLM prompt must: specify JSON output schema with stances array, require topic + direction + summary + evidence_quote, include explicit 'if uncertain, omit' instruction, extract only for named person. Read packages/core/src/integrations/conversations/extract.ts and packages/core/test/integrations/conversations/extract.test.ts before starting.
acceptanceCriteria: buildStancePrompt() tested independently — prompt includes person name, JSON schema, omission instruction
parseStanceResponse() tested independently — handles valid JSON, malformed JSON, missing fields, empty stances array, code-fenced responses
Integration test with mock LLMCallFn returning known stances — structured output matches PersonStance type
Mock LLM returning empty or uncertain produces graceful empty result (not error)
PersonStance and StanceDirection types exported from person-signals.ts
npm run typecheck && npm test pass

userStories.title: Build action item extraction with lifecycle
userStories.description: Add extractActionItemsForPerson(content, personName, ownerName?) and lifecycle functions to packages/core/src/services/person-signals.ts. Regex-based extraction matching patterns: '[Person] will [verb]', 'I'll send [person]', 'action item: [text]', 'TODO: [text]', '- [ ] [text]' near person name. Direction classification: owner name (from context/profile.md name field) in actor position = 'i_owe_them'; person name in actor position = 'they_owe_me'. Fallback: first-person language heuristics. Content-normalized hash for dedup: hash(lowercase(trimWhitespace(text)) + personSlug + direction). Lifecycle: auto-stale after 30 days, cap at 10 per direction (most recent kept), stale items excluded from meeting prep. Read packages/core/src/services/person-memory.ts for regex pattern examples and packages/core/src/services/entity.ts lines 1530-1555 for profile.md reading pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Extracts action items tied to specific people with correct direction
Direction classification correct with profile.md owner name
Falls back to first-person heuristics when no owner name provided
isActionItemStale() returns true for items older than 30 days
capActionItems() keeps at most 10 per direction (most recent)
deduplicateActionItems() prevents re-adding items with same hash
Re-extraction of same meeting content does not duplicate or resurrect stale items
Tests cover: happy path, aging, capping, dedup, no-owner-fallback, ambiguous actor edge case
npm run typecheck && npm test pass

userStories.title: Integrate extraction into refreshPersonMemory()
userStories.description: Update EntityService.refreshPersonMemory() to call stance and action item extraction from person-signals.ts. Add callLLM?: LLMCallFn to RefreshPersonMemoryOptions. Integration flow: (1) existing regex signals, (2) if callLLM provided call extractStancesForPerson with in-memory Map cache keyed by meetingPath:personName, (3) call extractActionItemsForPerson always, (4) read owner name from context/profile.md once per refresh, (5) aggregate stances with dedup, apply action item lifecycle, (6) pass all data to render. Without callLLM, only regex signals and action items run — stances skipped. Read packages/core/src/services/entity.ts refreshPersonMemory() method, packages/core/src/services/LEARNINGS.md for function-scoped Map cache pattern and SearchProvider invariants.
acceptanceCriteria: refreshPersonMemory() with callLLM in options produces stances alongside existing asks/concerns
Without callLLM, existing behavior identical — all current person-memory.test.ts tests pass unchanged
RefreshPersonMemoryOptions type has callLLM?: LLMCallFn field
RefreshPersonMemoryResult updated with stancesExtracted, actionItemsExtracted, itemsAgedOut counts
In-memory Map cache prevents duplicate LLM calls for same meeting+person within single refresh
Owner name read from profile.md once per refresh call, not per meeting
No new external dependencies added
npm run typecheck && npm test pass

userStories.title: Render enriched auto-memory sections
userStories.description: Extend renderPersonMemorySection() in person-memory.ts to include ### Stances, ### Open Items (I owe them), ### Open Items (They owe me), ### Relationship Health subsections inside the existing AUTO_PERSON_MEMORY:START/END block. Every stance and action item must have source citation (from: [Meeting Title], [Date]). Empty sections render as '- None detected yet.' consistent with existing pattern. Do NOT add new marker pairs — all content within the single existing AUTO_PERSON_MEMORY block. Read packages/core/src/services/person-memory.ts for current renderPersonMemorySection() implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Single auto-memory block contains all subsections (asks, concerns, stances, open items, health)
Source citations (from: Meeting Title, Date) on every stance and action item
Existing asks/concerns rendering unchanged (exact same format)
Empty sections render as '- None detected yet.'
Round-trip test: render then upsert then extract content and verify preserved
renderPersonMemorySection() signature updated to accept stances, action items, and health data
npm run typecheck && npm test pass

userStories.title: Compute relationship health metrics
userStories.description: Create packages/core/src/services/person-health.ts. Export computeRelationshipHealth(meetingDates: string[], openItemCount: number) returning RelationshipHealth type with lastMet, daysSinceLastMet, meetingsLast30Days, meetingsLast90Days, openLoopCount, and indicator (active/regular/cooling/dormant). Indicator thresholds: active = last meeting within 14 days, regular = within 30 days, cooling = within 60 days, dormant = 60+ days or never met. Pure computation, no I/O.
acceptanceCriteria: computeRelationshipHealth() returns correct RelationshipHealth for given meeting dates and open item count
Edge cases tested: empty array (never met = dormant), single meeting, daily meetings, boundary dates (exactly 14, 30, 60 days)
daysSinceLastMet computed correctly relative to current date
openLoopCount matches provided count
HealthIndicator and RelationshipHealth types exported
npm run typecheck && npm test pass

userStories.title: Update arete people show CLI + --dry-run
userStories.description: Update packages/cli/src/commands/people.ts: (1) arete people show <slug> --memory displays stances, open items, relationship health from auto-memory section, (2) add --dry-run flag to arete people memory refresh that shows extraction preview without writing, (3) update refresh summary to include extraction counts, (4) --json output adds new fields alongside existing (non-breaking). Read packages/cli/src/commands/people.ts and packages/cli/test/commands/people.test.ts before starting.
acceptanceCriteria: arete people show sarah --memory displays all enriched sections (stances, items, health)
--dry-run flag on refresh shows what would be extracted and writes nothing to disk
--json output includes stancesExtracted, actionItemsExtracted, itemsAgedOut alongside existing fields
Existing --json fields unchanged (backward compatible)
CLI tests added for dry-run flag and enriched display
npm run typecheck && npm test pass
